2. a is south.

4. If b is ivory, then b is south.
5. If a is south and a is violet, then b is willow.
6. If b is willow, then b is east.
7. If a is east and a is violet, then b is teal.
8. If b is teal, then b is north.
9. If b is east and b is willow, then c is ivory.
10. If c is ivory, then c is east.
11. If b is north and b is teal, then c is teal.
12. If c is teal, then c is south.
13. If b is south and b is ivory, then c is willow.
14. If c is willow, then c is north.
Which state applies to c?
</question>

<formal>
<constants>
a = a
b = b
c = c
</constants>
<predicates>
Ax: x is willow
Bx: x is ivory
Cx: x is teal
Dx: x is violet
Ex: x is north
Fx: x is south
Gx: x is east
</predicates>
<premises>
Da
Fa
Ea & Da -> Bb
Bb -> Fb
Fa & Da -> Ab
Ab -> Gb
Ga & Da -> Cb
Cb -> Eb
Gb & Ab -> Bc
Bc -> Gc
Eb & Cb -> Cc
Cc -> Fc
Fb & Bb -> Ac
Ac -> Ec
</premises>
<proof>
Da ; R
Fa ; R
Ab ; ->E
Gb ; ->E
Bc ; ->E
</proof>
<conclusion>
Bc
</conclusion>
</formal>
<answer>
ivory
</answer>
